Urea.name: Urea (46-0-0)
Urea.description: Urea is the most widely used nitrogen fertilizer in the world. It contains 46% nitrogen, making it the richest solid nitrogen source available.
Urea.application_method: Broadcast evenly over the field 2-3 days before irrigation. For standing crops, apply in splits — 50% at sowing, 25% at tillering, and 25% at panicle initiation.
Urea.warning: Do not apply urea with seeds directly, as it may cause seed burn. Avoid application during heavy rainfall to prevent nitrogen loss through leaching.

DAP.name: DAP (18-46-0)
DAP.description: Di-Ammonium Phosphate provides both nitrogen (18%) and phosphorus (46%). It is the most popular phosphatic fertilizer.
DAP.application_method: Apply as a basal dose during land preparation by drilling or broadcasting. Mix into the top 5-10 cm of soil. Best applied before sowing.
DAP.warning: Do not mix with alkaline fertilizers like calcium ammonium nitrate. Store in a dry place as DAP is hygroscopic.

MOP.name: MOP — Muriate of Potash (0-0-60)
MOP.description: Muriate of Potash (KCl) is the most common potassium fertilizer, containing 60% K₂O. Essential for disease resistance and water regulation in plants.
MOP.application_method: Apply as basal dose during final land preparation. For fruit crops, apply in a ring around the base of the plant. Split application recommended for sandy soils.
MOP.warning: Avoid over-application near germinating seeds. High chloride content may harm chloride-sensitive crops like tobacco and potatoes.

NPK 10-26-26.name: NPK Complex 10-26-26
NPK 10-26-26.description: A balanced complex fertilizer with 10% N, 26% P₂O₅, and 26% K₂O. Ideal for crops needing higher phosphorus and potassium with moderate nitrogen.
NPK 10-26-26.application_method: Apply as basal fertilizer during sowing. Can be drilled alongside the seed row at 5 cm depth. Supplement with urea for nitrogen top-dressing.
NPK 10-26-26.warning: Not suitable as a sole nitrogen source — always supplement with urea or ammonium sulphate for standing crops.

NPK 20-20-20.name: NPK Complex 20-20-20
NPK 20-20-20.description: A perfectly balanced water-soluble fertilizer providing equal parts nitrogen, phosphorus, and potassium. Excellent for fertigation and foliar spray.
NPK 20-20-20.application_method: Dissolve 3-5 grams per litre of water for foliar spray. For fertigation (drip), use 2-4 kg per acre per application cycle. Apply every 10-15 days during the growing season.
NPK 20-20-20.warning: Avoid spraying during peak sunshine hours to prevent leaf burn. Always test spray on a small area first.

Ammonium Sulphate.name: Ammonium Sulphate (21-0-0 + 24S)
Ammonium Sulphate.description: Contains 21% nitrogen and 24% sulphur. Ideal for sulphur-deficient soils and crops that need both N and S, such as oilseeds and pulses.
Ammonium Sulphate.application_method: Broadcast and incorporate into the soil. Can be side-dressed along crop rows. Apply 2-3 weeks after sowing for maximum benefit.
Ammonium Sulphate.warning: Acidifies soil over time. Not recommended for already acidic soils (pH < 5.5). Periodic liming may be needed with continued use.

SSP.name: SSP — Single Super Phosphate (0-16-0 + 11S + 21Ca)
SSP.description: Single Super Phosphate provides phosphorus (16%), sulphur (11%), and calcium (21%). It is the most economical source of phosphorus for Indian soils.
SSP.application_method: Apply as basal dose at the time of sowing. Broadcast and mix well into the soil. Best suited for acidic and neutral soils.
SSP.warning: Not suitable for alkaline soils (pH > 8). Bulkier than DAP — requires more quantity per acre for equivalent phosphorus.
